query.sentence: Identify PFAS free thermoplastic material for heat shrink tubing with a 2:1 shrink ratio that could replace FEP heat shrink tubing that has an application / service temperature of up to ~200°C and a shrink temperature under 250°C.

properties: Thermoplastic with melt flow suitable for extrusion
Glass transition temperature (Tg) ≥ 200 °C to allow service up to ~200 °C
Melting point or softening temperature ≤ 300 °C for lab‑scale processing
Shrink ratio of at least 2:1 when pre‑expanded and heated
Dimensional stability during heating (low shrinkage loss)
Chemical resistance to common solvents and cable insulation materials
Low moisture absorption for consistent dimensional control
constraints: Must be extrudable with standard twin‑screw or single‑screw extruders at < 350 °C
Cannot contain fluorinated monomers or additives
Shrink temperature must not exceed 250 °C to avoid cable damage
Material must retain mechanical integrity after repeated heating cycles

final_candidate.justification: PEN has a Tg of ~260 °C and a melt temperature around 280–310 °C, enabling service up to ~200 °C while remaining processable in standard extrusion equipment. Commercial PEN heat‑shrink tubing is available with pre‑expanded diameters that achieve a 2:1 shrink ratio when heated to 200–220 °C, well below the 250 °C limit. The polymer contains no fluorine atoms, satisfying the PFAS‑free requirement, and its high dimensional stability and chemical resistance make it suitable for cable insulation applications.

process_recipe.description: Extrude PEN pellets into a tube with the desired outer diameter (e.g., 0.5 in) and wall thickness (~0.02–0.04 in). Use a twin‑screw extruder set to 280–300 °C with a screw speed of 50–70 rpm.
process_recipe.conditions: Feed rate 1–2 kg/h, barrel temperature profile 260/270/280/290/300 °C (from feed zone to die).

process_recipe.description: Pre‑expand the freshly extruded tube by heating it in a convection oven at 120–140 °C for 5 minutes. This creates an expansion ratio of ~1.8–2.0, which will contract to the original diameter when later heated.
process_recipe.conditions: Oven temperature 130 °C, dwell time 5 min, air flow 1 m/s.

process_recipe.description: Cool the expanded tube to room temperature and store in a dry environment to prevent moisture uptake.
For use, place the tube over the cable or component and heat in a laboratory oven or hot air gun at 200–220 °C for 30–60 s. The tube will contract to its original diameter, providing a tight fit.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature 210 °C, dwell time 45 s, airflow 0.5 m/s.
process_recipe.equipment_class: Hot air oven or hot‑air gun
